title: loxanthera corymbosa
intro: Loxanthera corymbosa, also known as the starfruit bush, is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and roots of the plant are used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, which are believed to offer health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving headaches, and supporting immune function. Furthermore, Loxanthera corymbosa has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
